transcript.content: Please close T-344 with a warning and repair plan for Ana Torres, plus a clear reply and memo.

{'customer': 'Ana Torres', 'priority': 'high', 'subject': 'Ana Torres / clinic badge loan', 'body': 'Ana lent her visitor badge to her spouse for five minutes at the clinic entrance. No restricted area was accessed, and she told security before the shift ended.'}

args.body:
Hi Ana,

Thank you for self-reporting the badge loan to security before your shift ended — that transparency matters and is reflected in how we're closing this out.

That said, lending a clinic visitor badge to anyone, including a spouse, is a credentialing policy violation regardless of duration or whether a restricted area was entered. Badges are issued to a single named individual and must not be transferred. We are closing this matter with the following:

1) Formal written warning placed in your file for badge sharing.
2) Repair plan:
   - Re-complete the Credentialing & Access Control training module within 7 days.
   - Acknowledge the badge-use policy in writing upon completion.
   - 90-day review period; any further credentialing issue during this window will escalate to a higher-level disciplinary step.
   - Your spouse should be signed in as a visitor through the front desk in the future, not via your badge.

No further action is required from you today beyond scheduling the training. Please reach out if you have questions.

Thank you,
Operations

Incident: Ana Torres lent her clinic visitor badge to her spouse for ~5 minutes at the clinic entrance. No restricted area accessed. Ana self-disclosed to security before end of shift.

Policy: Badge sharing/transfer violates credentialing and access control policy regardless of duration or area accessed.

Disposition: Formal written warning issued. Mitigating factor: voluntary self-report and no restricted access.

Repair plan:
- Retake Credentialing & Access Control training within 7 days
- Written acknowledgment of badge-use policy upon completion
- 90-day review period; any recurrence escalates to next disciplinary tier
- Future visitor (incl. spouse) entries to go through front-desk visitor sign-in

Ticket T-344 closed.

transcript.content: memo filed to Ana Torres - T-344 (734 chars)

args.resolution: Closed with formal written warning for badge sharing and a 90-day repair plan (retake credentialing training within 7 days, written policy acknowledgment, monitoring period). Self-report noted as mitigating. Memo filed; customer notified.

args.summary: T-344 closed: sent reply to Ana Torres outlining the written warning and repair plan, filed an internal memo documenting the incident, mitigating self-report, and 90-day plan, and closed the ticket with a resolution note.
